paper_title: Aerobiological Study of Lime Pollen Concentration in Poland

methods.description: Process for collecting lime pollen samples from the air using Hirst-type devices to measure pollen concentration in Lublin and Szczecin, Poland.

steps.description: Hirst-type devices (Lanzoni VPPS 2000) were used to collect pollen samples from the air.
steps.searchable_summary: use Hirst-type devices to collect air pollen samples

methods.description: Method to determine the start and end dates of the pollen season using the 98% method, based on cumulative pollen concentration.

steps.description: The 98% method was used to determine the pollen season dates.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 98% method to determine pollen season dates

methods.description: Analysis of various parameters of the pollen season, including start, end, duration, peak concentration, and annual pollen sum.

steps.description: The following parameters of the pollen season were analyzed: start, end, and duration; maximum pollen concentration (peak value); date of the maximum concentration; and annual pollen sum.

methods.description: Analysis of the relationship between pollen season dynamics and meteorological factors using Spearman's correlation.

steps.description: The relationships between the course of the season and selected meteorological factors (mean temperature, humidity, and rainfall) were determined using Spearman's correlation analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Spearman's correlation analysis with meteorological data

methods.description: Calculation of cumulative effective temperatures to forecast the beginning of pollen seasons for Tilia in Lublin and Szczecin.

steps.description: Cumulative (effective) temperatures for Tilia growing in Lublin and Szczecin were calculated by summing temperatures of >0°C and >5.5°C from January 1 and March 1 each year to the day of the onset of the pollen season.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cumulative temperatures for pollen season forecasting
